page: Amazon Web Services r4.2xlarge is a memory-optimized fourth-generation server instance. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The configuration includes 8 virtual CPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB of RAM per core. The CPU features a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

This instance operates on the Xen hypervisor. It does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage must be network-attached. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the r4.2xlarge is positioned as a legacy or lower-tier compute option. Single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, rank in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth performance is mixed; while small block read-only and large block write operations score in the weak tier, large block read-only memory bandwidth achieves an average rating. Database operations and Redis SET throughput are relatively low, with Redis performance falling into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r4.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the premium cost of newer generation hardware, making it a viable option for memory-intensive legacy workloads that do not require high compute density. It is best suited for medium-sized in-memory caches, database replicas, and general-purpose memory-intensive applications where raw CPU performance and local storage speeds are not primary bottlenecks.
description: Amazon Web Services r4.2xlarge is a memory-optimized, fourth-generation instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 61.0 GB of RAM. The instance operates on the Xen hypervisor, provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data indicates weak to poor CPU performance, with single-core and multi-core scores falling in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, its large-block read-only memory bandwidth and small-model text generation speeds achieve average ratings. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and modest compute capabilities, the r4.2xlarge is best suited for memory-intensive legacy applications, database caching, and moderate data processing tasks where high-speed local storage and raw processing power are not critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r4.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 61.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, suited for legacy memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized legacy instance featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute performance.

61.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Xen hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-intensive legacy applications and database caching
